# Literature index

### Collision-rate / self-broadening series

| [`bala2026`](lit/bala2026.md) | VERIFIED | — | M4 | Isotope-dependence-of-collisional-width framework: why beta\_85=beta\_87 is expected (thermal impact, tiny reduced-mass difference). |
| [`baranger1958`](lit/baranger1958.md) | VERIFIED | — | methods/02 | The impact approximation cited in docs/methods/02\_the\_lineshape.md Sec.\ 2.2: uncorrelated, phase-interrupting collisions add a rate to the coherence decay, keeping the line Lorentzian with a density-linear width — textbook basis for gamma\_coll = beta\_self * N. |
| [`sautenkov2026`](lit/sautenkov2026.md) | VERIFIED | — | M4 | Rb D2 RESONANCE-line self-broadening (static+collision width): the resonance-vs-vdW contrast for why our S->S self term is small. |
| [`spiegelman2022`](lit/spiegelman2022.md) | VERIFIED | — | — | Allard-Kielkopf broadening lineage (quasistatic satellites). |

### Transit-time lineshape pedigree

| citekey | status | routing | loci | why it matters |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [`biraben1979`](lit/biraben1979.md) | VERIFIED | — | M3, M9, methods/02 | Origin of our transit kernel: the finite-transit Doppler-free two-photon line is a Lorentzian convolved with a two-sided exponential (the central cusp). |
| [`borde1976`](lit/borde1976.md) | VERIFIED | — | methods/02 | The earlier, more general transit-time treatment that Biraben~1979 and Lehmann~2021 both simplify. |
| [`lehmann2021`](lit/lehmann2021.md) | VERIFIED | — | M8, M9, constants, methods/02 | Modern closed-form transit-limit lineshape (the ``Lehmann lineshape''). |

### This line (5S-6S, 993 nm): OIST apparatus lineage

| citekey | status | routing | loci | why it matters |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [`gokhroo2022`](lit/gokhroo2022.md) | VERIFIED | — | P1, P2, methods/02 | Read in full 2026-07-16: cold ⁸⁷Rb in a MOT around the fibre (NOT a warm vapour, as an earlier draft of ours said), driving 5S₁/₂ (F=2) → 6S₁/₂ (F″=2) at 993 nm — our exact line — with an M Squared SolsTis Ti:Sapph quoted at sub-MHz linewidth, and a counter-propagating 993 nm vapour cell as the Doppler-free frequency reference. |
| [`nieddu2019`](lit/nieddu2019.md) | VERIFIED | — | M7, P1, P2, constants, methods/02 | OIST apparatus lineage: measured 1/e^2 beam DIAMETER 128 um (w0=64 um, f=150 mm) — corroborates our w0 re-pin. |
| [`rajasree2020`](lit/rajasree2020.md) | VERIFIED | — | constants, methods/02 | Confirms the 128 um beam + Steck N(T) chain. |
| [`rajasree2020spin`](lit/rajasree2020spin.md) | VERIFIED | — | M10, P2 | OUR line, two configs: warm vapor paraxial (rate proportional to the squared degree of linear polarization, ZERO for circular — the polarization amplitude law and the fixed-lock session extinction null-test) and cold 87Rb around a 400 nm ONF (evanescent-field 5S-6S at 25-40 counts/ms — Paper-2 feasibility evidence. |
| [`ray2020`](lit/ray2020.md) | VERIFIED | — | — | Group lineage; title/journal VERIFY. |
| [`roy2017`](lit/roy2017.md) | VERIFIED | — | — | Group lineage; title/journal VERIFY. |

| [`safronovaSS2011`](lit/safronovaSS2011.md) | VERIFIED | FEED | M16, THEORY | The critically-evaluated Rb ns/np/nd matrix elements + scalar/tensor static polarizabilities (the S and S 2011 lineage our polarizability elements descend from), and the independent source to validate the M16 7S static (~3.2e4 a.u.). |
| [`snadden1996`](lit/snadden1996.md) | VERIFIED | — | M2 | FM two-photon in cold Rb — EOM-ruler ancestry (with Zapka 1983, Bjorklund 1980). |
| [`steck_rb`](lit/steck_rb.md) | REPORTED | — | M4, constants, methods/02, methods/03 | Reference atomic constants for Rb (line frequencies, natural widths, polarizabilities). |
| [`yang2025`](lit/yang2025.md) | VERIFIED | FEED | M2 | Integrated LiNbO3 electro-optic combs track a laser's instantaneous optical frequency in real time (chirp to 2e18 Hz/s) — the modern frontier of the internal-ruler-tracks-a-drifting-axis idea our EOM ruler embodies. |

### ONF community map and near-surface physics (Paper 2)

| citekey | status | routing | loci | why it matters |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [`beard2024`](lit/beard2024.md) | VERIFIED | CITE | — | First 5S-5D5/2 two-photon Rb clock read out on the 776 nm (5D-to-6P) cascade fluorescence — a second cascade-detection precedent for PLAN 8.4a. |
| [`dutta2024`](lit/dutta2024.md) | VERIFIED | FEED | P2 | Quadrupole/octupole CP corrections when the atomic wavefunction is not small vs atom-surface distance, and their imprint on thin-cell / selective-reflection lineshapes. |
| [`dutta2025`](lit/dutta2025.md) | VERIFIED | CITE | P2:§IV | Folds the finite thermal (Doppler) velocity distribution into the CP selective-reflection lineshape (beyond the infinite-Doppler approximation) — the direct template for reading the near-surface shift as a thermal-averaged distribution in Paper 2 sec IV. |
| [`hassanin2023`](lit/hassanin2023.md) | VERIFIED | CITE | M7 | Detects the 5D-to-5P INFRARED cascade to observe the Doppler-free 5S-5D two-photon line without reabsorption at high density — the direct precedent for our 1.3 um (6S-to-5P) trapping-free amplitude channel (PLAN 8.4a). |
| [`pache2026`](lit/pache2026.md) | VERIFIED | FEED | P2 | EIT-cooling / atom-source thread (companion to pennetta2026, same group + 450 nm fibre). |
| [`pennetta2026`](lit/pennetta2026.md) | VERIFIED | FEED | P2 | nearest-platform for Paper 2. |
| [`piotrowski2026`](lit/piotrowski2026.md) | VERIFIED | CITE | P2 | Probe-light scattering heats nanophotonic-trapped atoms, so near-field probing is inherently transient (coupling + atom-number decay). |
| [`sargsyan2025`](lit/sargsyan2025.md) | VERIFIED | CITE | P2:§IV | Extracts the atom-surface red shift (estimates C3) of the Cs 6S-7P line from a nanocell selective-reflection lineshape (below 400 nm thickness) — the experimental analogue of reading a surface shift from the line for Paper 2 sec IV. |
| [`ton2026`](lit/ton2026.md) | VERIFIED | CITE | P2:§IV | Reads the kHz Casimir-Polder shift of ultracold Sr 189 nm from a dielectric out of the spectroscopic lineshape, across the near-field-to- retarded crossover. |
| [`wang2025`](lit/wang2025.md) | VERIFIED | CITE | M16, M7 | Experimental multi-channel cascade fluorescence of the Rb 5S-7S two-photon line (760 nm) at 780/741/795/728/~420 nm, branching-consistent, vs power/polarization/temperature. |
